_meta.purpose: Fills the topics genuinely absent from the ciso-toolkit (MIT) + imported policy sets: threat & vulnerability management, corporate governance, data classification & handling, and mobile device management / BYOD. Authored in IC house style; framework refs live in policy-template-framework-map.json keyed by these externalRefs.

templates.title: Threat & Vulnerability Management Policy
templates.contentText:
# Threat & Vulnerability Management Policy

## 1. Purpose
To ensure that threats and technical vulnerabilities affecting the organization's systems, applications, and services are identified, assessed, prioritised, and remediated in a timely, risk-based manner before they can be exploited.

## 2. Scope
This policy applies to all information systems, cloud services, networks, endpoints, and applications owned or operated by the organization, and to all personnel responsible for their configuration, operation, or security.

## 3. Policy Statements

### 3.1 Threat Intelligence
- Relevant sources of threat intelligence shall be monitored to stay informed of emerging threats, exploits, and adversary techniques.
- Actionable intelligence shall be evaluated against the organization's technology footprint and fed into vulnerability prioritisation.

### 3.2 Vulnerability Identification
- Authenticated vulnerability scans shall be run on a defined, recurring schedule and after significant change.
- Software composition and dependency analysis shall be used to surface vulnerabilities in third-party and open-source components.
- Externally reported vulnerabilities shall be accepted through a documented intake channel.

### 3.3 Assessment & Prioritisation
- Each vulnerability shall be assigned a severity based on exploitability, exposure, and business impact — not the raw scanner score alone.
- Remediation timeframes shall be set by severity (for example: critical addressed fastest), with the target windows recorded and tracked.

### 3.4 Remediation & Verification
- Vulnerabilities shall be remediated by patching, configuration change, or compensating control within their target window.
- Where a fix cannot be applied in time, a documented risk acceptance with an owner and expiry shall be recorded.
- Remediation shall be verified by re-scan or test before the item is closed.

### 3.5 Metrics & Reporting
- Open vulnerabilities, overdue items, and remediation timeliness shall be reported to management on a regular cadence.

## 4. Responsibilities
- **Security Team**: Runs scans, triages findings, sets severities, and tracks remediation to closure.
- **System & Application Owners**: Remediate assigned vulnerabilities within target windows and evidence the fix.
- **Senior Management**: Reviews vulnerability posture and approves risk acceptances that exceed defined thresholds.

## 5. Review
This policy shall be reviewed at least annually and updated in response to significant changes in the threat landscape or technology estate.

templates.title: Corporate Governance of Information Security Policy
templates.contentText:
# Corporate Governance of Information Security Policy

## 1. Purpose
To define how the organization's governing body directs, oversees, and remains accountable for information security, ensuring security objectives are aligned with business strategy and adequately resourced.

## 2. Scope
This policy applies to the board or equivalent governing body, senior management, and all functions that own or execute security-related decisions across the organization.

## 3. Policy Statements

### 3.1 Accountability
- The governing body holds ultimate accountability for information security and shall not delegate that accountability, even where operational responsibility is assigned.
- A named member of senior management shall be responsible for the security programme and shall report to the governing body.

### 3.2 Direction & Objectives
- The governing body shall approve the information security policy framework and set measurable security objectives aligned to business goals and risk appetite.
- Security shall be considered in strategic, investment, and change decisions.

### 3.3 Oversight
- The governing body shall receive regular reporting on risk posture, incidents, compliance status, and programme performance.
- Independent assurance (internal audit or external review) shall be commissioned periodically to validate control effectiveness.

### 3.4 Resourcing & Competence
- Adequate budget, staffing, and tooling shall be provided to meet approved security objectives.
- Members of the governing body shall maintain sufficient security awareness to exercise informed oversight.

### 3.5 Culture
- The governing body shall promote a culture in which security is a shared responsibility and concerns can be raised without fear of reprisal.

## 4. Responsibilities
- **Governing Body**: Sets direction, approves policy and objectives, and provides oversight and resources.
- **Senior Management**: Executes the programme, manages risk, and reports to the governing body.
- **All Functions**: Operate within the approved framework and escalate material risks.

## 5. Review
This policy shall be reviewed at least annually, and whenever there is a significant change to the organization's structure, strategy, or regulatory obligations.

templates.title: Data Classification & Handling Policy
templates.contentText:
# Data Classification & Handling Policy

## 1. Purpose
To ensure information is classified according to its sensitivity and value, and handled with protective measures appropriate to its classification throughout its lifecycle.

## 2. Scope
This policy applies to all information created, received, stored, processed, or transmitted by the organization, in any format, and to everyone who handles that information.

## 3. Policy Statements

### 3.1 Classification Scheme
- Information shall be classified into defined levels (for example: Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted) based on the impact of unauthorised disclosure, alteration, or loss.
- The owner of an information asset is responsible for assigning and maintaining its classification.

### 3.2 Labelling
- Information and its containers shall be labelled in line with their classification where technically and operationally feasible.
- Default handling shall assume the Internal level where a label is absent.

### 3.3 Handling Rules
- Each classification level shall have documented handling rules covering storage, access, transmission, sharing, printing, and disposal.
- Higher classifications shall require stronger controls, including encryption of Restricted and Confidential data in transit and at rest.
- Sharing of Confidential or Restricted information with third parties shall require an approved agreement and a business need.

### 3.4 Retention & Disposal
- Information shall be retained only as long as required by business, legal, or regulatory need, then securely disposed of.
- Disposal methods shall render information unrecoverable in a manner proportionate to its classification.

### 3.5 Reclassification
- Classifications shall be reviewed when information changes in sensitivity and adjusted accordingly.

## 4. Responsibilities
- **Information Owners**: Classify assets, define handling needs, and review classifications.
- **All Staff**: Handle information according to its classification and report mishandling.
- **Security Team**: Maintains the classification scheme and handling standards and monitors adherence.

## 5. Review
This policy shall be reviewed at least annually and updated to reflect changes in legal, regulatory, or business requirements.

templates.title: Mobile Device Management & BYOD Policy
templates.contentText:
# Mobile Device Management & BYOD Policy

## 1. Purpose
To protect organizational information accessed from mobile and personally owned (BYOD) devices by defining the security requirements for enrolling, managing, and using such devices.

## 2. Scope
This policy applies to all smartphones, tablets, and laptops — whether organization-owned or personally owned — used to access organizational information, and to all users of those devices.

## 3. Policy Statements

### 3.1 Enrolment & Eligibility
- Devices used to access organizational information shall be enrolled in the mobile device management (MDM) solution before access is granted.
- BYOD access shall require the user's acceptance of these terms, including the organization's right to enforce controls and remotely wipe organizational data.

### 3.2 Baseline Controls
- Enrolled devices shall enforce screen lock, strong authentication, device encryption, and current operating-system and application patch levels.
- Jailbroken or rooted devices shall be denied access to organizational information.

### 3.3 Separation of Data
- On BYOD devices, organizational data shall be held within a managed container or profile, kept separate from personal data.
- Organizational data shall not be copied into unmanaged personal apps or storage.

### 3.4 Loss, Theft & Offboarding
- Lost or stolen devices shall be reported immediately so organizational data can be revoked or wiped.
- Upon a user's departure or loss of eligibility, organizational data and access shall be removed from the device.

### 3.5 Privacy
- Management controls shall be limited to what is necessary to protect organizational information; personal content on BYOD devices shall not be monitored beyond that purpose.

## 4. Responsibilities
- **IT / Security Team**: Operates the MDM solution, defines the baseline, and enforces compliance and wipe actions.
- **Device Users**: Keep devices compliant, protect credentials, and report loss or compromise promptly.
- **Senior Management**: Approves the acceptable-use terms and eligibility criteria for BYOD.

## 5. Review
This policy shall be reviewed at least annually and updated in response to changes in device technology, threats, or workforce practices.
